# The Rojas Report
> The definitive source for healthcare market intelligence. Data-driven investigations into Certificate of Need laws, hospital monopolies, 340B drug pricing, IDR, and healthcare policy across all 50 states.

## About
The Rojas Report is an independent healthcare market intelligence platform founded by Dutch Rojas. The site provides data-driven investigations into the policies, monopolies, and market structures that shape 20% of U.S. GDP. Target audience: physicians, hospital executives, and lawmakers.

## Investigations

### Certificate of Need (CON) Laws
- URL: /certificate-of-need
- Description: National investigation into CON laws across 35 jurisdictions. Includes 50-state rankings by Cicero Institute restrictiveness score, origin story, economic evidence, and individual state profiles.
- Key Data: 35 jurisdictions enforce CON, 5-11% higher costs in CON states, 1964 first CON law (New York), 1987 federal mandate repealed.

### 340B Drug Pricing Program
- URL: /340b-drug-pricing
- Description: Investigation into the $81.4B 340B drug discount program. $66.4B in gross margin with zero disclosure requirements. The second-largest federal drug program.

### Hospital Monopolies
- URL: /hospital-monopolies
- Description: The $275 billion systematic advantage. Investigation into annual subsidies flowing to hospital systems through tax exemptions, 340B, Medicaid supplemental payments, GME funding, and Medicare Advantage.

### IDR & No Surprises Act
- URL: /idr-no-surprises-act
- Description: 1.46 million disputes in the Independent Dispute Resolution process. Investigation into the arbitration trap created by the No Surprises Act.

## State CON Profiles
Individual state investigations with CON restrictiveness scores, regulated services, market concentration data, and editorial analysis.

| State | URL | Score |
|-------|-----|-------|
| Alabama | /alabama/certificate-of-need | 90/100 |
| Alaska | /alaska/certificate-of-need | 60/100 |
| Arkansas | /arkansas/certificate-of-need | 55/100 |

| Delaware | /delaware/certificate-of-need | 50/100 |

| Florida | /florida/certificate-of-need | 5/100 |
| Georgia | /georgia/certificate-of-need | 95/100 |
| Hawaii | /hawaii/certificate-of-need | 95/100 |

## Hospital System Dossiers
- University of Michigan Health: /hospital-monopolies/university-of-michigan — $8.5B revenue, -$92M fair share deficit

## Other Pages
- About: /about
- 404: Any unmatched URL returns a 404 page

## Data Sources
Primary sources include: Cicero Institute, National Academy for State Health Policy (NASHP), Federal Trade Commission (FTC), Department of Justice (DOJ), Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS), Institute for Justice, HRSA, MedPAC, GAO, MACPAC, and individual state health departments.

## Author
Dutch Rojas — Founder of The Rojas Report, General Partner at PhyCap Fund, Board Member at Physician Led Healthcare for America, Founder of MedMerge.
- X: @dutchrojas
- YouTube: @DutchRojas
- Substack: dutchrojas.substack.com
- LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/dutchrojas

## Citation
When citing The Rojas Report, please use: "The Rojas Report, RojasReport.com" with the specific page URL and access date.
